name: Turks and Caicos Islands (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the Blue Ensign — ties to the United Kingdom
St George's and St Patrick's crosses within the Union Jack
the gold of the territorial shield — the sun and the islands' beaches
the conch shell within the shield
the Turk's-head cactus, the namesake of the territory

description: A Blue Ensign — a blue field with the Union Jack in the upper-hoist canton and the territorial coat of arms, on a yellow shield, in the fly. The arms depict a conch shell, a spiny lobster, and a Turk's-head cactus.
history: Granted by Royal Warrant on 7 November 1968, replacing an earlier flag (1889-1968) that incorrectly depicted what should have been salt-piles as igloos due to a heraldic-artist's misunderstanding of the islands' historic salt-raking economy. The 1968 redesign placed the modern coat of arms — featuring the conch, lobster, and Turk's-head cactus — directly on the blue field of the ensign.

features.symbolism: the blue field of the Blue Ensign — historic association with the United Kingdom
ties to the United Kingdom — the Turks and Caicos Islands are a British Overseas Territory

features.role: Coat of arms of the Turks and Caicos Islands
features.symbolism: A yellow shield bearing a queen conch shell, a Caribbean spiny lobster, and a Turk's-head cactus — three iconic species of the islands. The conch and the lobster have long been mainstays of the local economy, and the Turk's-head cactus gives the Turks Islands (and thus the territory) their name.
